tossups.question: One of the two versions of this painting features intricately rendered Stars-of-Bethlehem’s and heartsease flowers in its background, reflecting its artist love of botany. That version of this painting was thought to be part of an altarpiece for that was flanked by a depiction of a green angel by Bernardino Luini, and a depiction of a red angel holding a lute by Ambrogio de Predis. The version of this painting in London’s National Gallery features Mary wearing a (*) dark blue robe and references Christ’s death by being set in a grotto. Both versions of these paintings, which make use of sfumato, feature an angel, an infant John the Baptist, and an infant Jesus. The Louvre contains the more famous version of, for 10 points, what painting by Leonardo da Vinci, which depicts the Virgin and Child in a rocky landscape?
tossups.answer: The Madonna of the Rocks or Virgin of the Rocks
tossups.answer_sanitized: The Madonna of the Rocks or Virgin of the Rocks
tossups.question_sanitized: One of the two versions of this painting features intricately rendered Stars-of-Bethlehem's and heartsease flowers in its background, reflecting its artist love of botany. That version of this painting was thought to be part of an altarpiece for that was flanked by a depiction of a green angel by Bernardino Luini, and a depiction of a red angel holding a lute by Ambrogio de Predis. The version of this painting in London's National Gallery features Mary wearing a (*) dark blue robe and references Christ's death by being set in a grotto. Both versions of these paintings, which make use of sfumato, feature an angel, an infant John the Baptist, and an infant Jesus. The Louvre contains the more famous version of, for 10 points, what painting by Leonardo da Vinci, which depicts the Virgin and Child in a rocky landscape?

bonuses.leadin: Leonardo wrote several treatises in preparation for a colossal sculpture depicting this artistic subject; that sculpture was never completed after the seventy tons of bronze needed were used to build cannons instead. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artistic subject, several of which were also depicted in a painting called “The Lost Leonardo.” That painting featuring several of these subjects was accidentally melted after charcoal stoves were used to try to dry it.

Leonardo’s unrealized horse sculpture was abandoned in favor of defending this city against a siege by Charles VIII. The horse was intended for this city’s ruler Ludovico Sforza, who also patronized Leonardo’s The Last Supper.

Leonardo’s Milan period produced the two versions of this painting. Both the Louvre and the British National Gallery version show the angel Uriel and John the Baptist as part of a triangular composition in the title setting.
answers: horses [or equestrian art; prompt on riders or soldiers by asking “what animal are they riding?”; prompt on battles or fights by asking “what creatures are involved in the fight?”] (The painting is Leonardo da Vinci’s The Battle of Anghiari.)
Virgin of the Rocks [or Madonna of the Rocks; accept anything with Virgin or Madonna and rocks]

Virgin of the Rocks [or Madonna of the Rocks; accept anything with Virgin or Madonna and rocks]

Leonardo's unrealized horse sculpture was abandoned in favor of defending this city against a siege by Charles VIII. The horse was intended for this city's ruler Ludovico Sforza, who also patronized Leonardo's The Last Supper.

Leonardo's Milan period produced the two versions of this painting. Both the Louvre and the British National Gallery version show the angel Uriel and John the Baptist as part of a triangular composition in the title setting.
